title: Fix machine volumes with long path
body:
AppleHV accepts a max 36 bytes for mount tags. Instead of using the fully qualified path for the mount tag, use the sha256 of the path. 

Fixes: #22226
Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22505

<!--

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note

requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Fixed a bug where podman machine start would fail if the machine had a volume with a long target path. 
Fixed a bug where  podman machine mounted paths that included dashes in the wrong location.
```

comments.body:
@Luap99 Turns out this fix doesn't work - the tag is actually the path that gets mounted inside the VM. 

I think for now a quick fix, something like throwing an error might work, and that would buy us time for a long-term solution? WDYT?
I think this has to be fixed properly? Why would we mount the tag?
It's the AppleHV specification I believe, they use the tag as the destination path inside the VM
the tag should be the source, virtiofs should be mounted with something like `mount -t virtiofs TAG DEST`, well in theory I don't have a mac to check how this works with vfkit/apple hypervisor.
@Luap99 Alright, let me check one more time and make sure I'm not confusing something, will get back to you!

> the tag should be the source, virtiofs should be mounted with something like `mount -t virtiofs TAG DEST`, well in theory I don't have a mac to check how this works with vfkit/apple hypervisor.

This is what podman-machine appears to be doing https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/9ac6d9de45ab18591a2bc38994ebda1d0d2bd2d0/pkg/machine/apple/apple.go#L86-L106 , mount `$TAG` to `$TARGET`
Ah I think the issue is that the tag is used as unit name which is incorrect, the name must be the target escaped according to systemd rules (https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22505)
@Luap99 @cfergeau Thanks for pointing that out! I'll try that fix once I'm back on Mac after lunch :)
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
@Luap99 @cfergeau Tested the solution locally and it worked! TYVM, PTAnotherL

When running podman machine start I'm getting the error: `Error: vfkit exited unexpectedly with exit code 1` **macOS Big Sur 11.7.10** I tried following this github thread to see if a resolution was found. Not sure why I'm getting the error. My vfkit version is: vfkit version: **0.5.1** I tried changing the provider to qemu using the environment variable: CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER but that came back with an error when trying to list the machines: elliot@Elliot-Mac-BigSur:~$ podman machine list Error: unsupported virtualization provider: `qemu` Also I ran podman machine reset - in hopes that would solve the issue but it did not. Also I apologize if this is the wrong forum for my issue. I apologize in advance is this is not the right forum for my issue.
